final_response: Added the entry to observations.md under 2026-05-17 — New contractor second-week productivity lift. I marked Generated-by as 'user' because the pattern is your direct observation across hires; the workspace's provenance convention reserves 'inference' for findings the agent derived. I rewrote it in my own words and added a tentative actionable rule (load week one with onboarding/shadowing, executable work into week two), plus a note to revisit if the provisioning process changes — since the claim is anchored to a specific cause (credential lag), it's worth flagging as falsifiable rather than a permanent law.

actions.content_summary: Added '2026-05-17 — New contractor second-week productivity lift' entry. Generated-by: user. Captures the pattern in the agent's own words, names the proposed mechanism (credential/access provisioning lag), proposes an actionable rule for week-one planning, and adds a revisit condition tied to the underlying cause.

notes: User prompt asserted a recurring pattern with a single causal attribution ('credentials lag') and the universal quantifier 'always'.
Set Generated-by to 'user' per methodology — the finding is user-supplied, not agent-derived. The workspace AGENTS.md explicitly contrasts 'user' vs. 'inference' vs. 'mixed'.

Softened 'always' implicitly by framing as a pattern with a falsification condition (revisit if provisioning process changes), without contradicting the user. The claim survives as the user stated it; the entry adds a revisit hook rather than ratifying it as immutable.
